metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC #3 (e.g., 'push a synthetic refs/heads/feature/smoke-test branch to the mirror repo and confirm via `gh run list --branch feature/smoke-test` that zero workflow runs are triggered for test.yml, eval.yml, and optionality.yml').
findings.finding: AC #3 gestures at '(evidence)' but neither the AC nor the What section specifies a concrete proving command or procedure for demonstrating zero push-triggered mirror runs — leaving the verifier to decide how to gather that evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3: 'A feature/* branch push produces zero push-triggered mirror runs (evidence)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #3 to read: 'A feature/* branch push produces zero push-triggered mirror runs — verified by pushing a synthetic branch (e.g. feature/smoke-test) to the mirror and running `gh run list --branch feature/smoke-test --repo <mirror>` to confirm no runs for test.yml, eval.yml, or optionality.yml appear.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the glob pattern used in all three ACs and the What section to one consistent form (e.g. 'feature/**' everywhere) and confirm it matches the intended branch naming convention.
findings.finding: Glob pattern inconsistency between the What section ('feature/**') and AC1/AC3 ('feature/*') creates ambiguity about whether nested branch names like 'feature/epic/foo' are in scope for exclusion.
findings.location: What section ('feature/**') vs AC1 ('refs/heads/feature/*') vs AC3 ('feature/* branch')
findings.suggested_fix: Standardise to 'feature/**' in AC1 and AC3 ('no longer run on pushes to refs/heads/feature/**') to match the branches-ignore example in the What section, unless single-level feature/* branches are the only ones replicated (in which case document that constraint explicitly).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the glob pattern across the plan: pick either `feature/*` or `feature/**`, match it to the replication.config refspec (`feature/*`), and use the same pattern consistently in the 'What' example and all three ACs.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'feature/**' (double-glob, matches nested paths like feature/epic/foo) in the 'What' section example ('branches-ignore: ["feature/**"]') but uses 'feature/*' (single-glob, one level only) in AC1 and AC3. The replication.config refspec 'refs/heads/feature/*:refs/heads/feature/*' uses single-glob, meaning only single-level feature branch names are replicated. Applying 'feature/**' in the workflow files would be broader than what is actually replicated, and is inconsistent with the AC wording — creating ambiguity about which pattern should actually be implemented.
findings.location: What section ('feature/**') vs AC1/AC3 ('feature/*') vs infra/gerrit/replication.config line 78
findings.suggested_fix: Use `feature/*` (single-glob) everywhere in the plan to match the `push = refs/heads/feature/*:refs/heads/feature/*` refspec in replication.config, which defines exactly what gets replicated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the 'What' section explaining why `branches-ignore` at the push-trigger level is preferred (e.g., it is the idiomatic GitHub Actions mechanism, applies once per workflow rather than per-job, automatically preserves `pull_request`/`workflow_dispatch`/`schedule` triggers, and avoids any risk of accidentally touching the gerrit-verify path).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for WHY `branches-ignore` at the push-trigger level is the preferred mechanism — it describes what to do and why the problem exists, but omits why this approach fits (e.g., idiomatic GitHub Actions pattern, no per-job duplication, preserves all non-push triggers automatically, no risk of accidentally gating the Verified path).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'This trigger-level filter is preferred over per-job `if:` conditions because it applies once per workflow file, composes cleanly with GitHub Actions' branch-filter system, and automatically leaves `pull_request`, `workflow_dispatch`, and `schedule` triggers untouched.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving step for AC #2: confirm (e.g. via a dry-run YAML lint or by opening a test PR against main on the mirror) that push-to-main and pull_request triggers for test.yml, eval.yml, and optionality.yml remain active after the branches-ignore change.
findings.finding: AC #2 ('main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected') has no mapped step or proving command to demonstrate that existing main-branch and PR-triggered runs still fire correctly after the branches-ignore addition.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #2: 'main + PR triggers unchanged; gerrit-verify Verified path unaffected'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
